issues.description: Claim 'each pump cycles roughly a hundred times every second' is not in the reference. Reference only states the 3 Na+ out / 2 K+ in per ATP stoichiometry; no cycling rate is given.
Claim 'your nerve cells carry millions of them' (pumps per nerve cell) is not in the reference.
Claim 'around twenty percent of your resting energy budget' is not in the reference. No energy-budget figure appears in the M3 section.
Claim 'if it stopped for even a few minutes, you would lose consciousness' is not in the reference.

Charged species 'calcium, chloride' are added beyond reference, which lists only 'Na+, K+' as ion examples (line 135-136). Generalization to all ions is consistent with the reference statement that 'charged species ... require transport proteins,' but the specific Ca2+/Cl- examples are unverified within this section.
'Antiporters carry them opposite directions' — the reference mentions only symporters (Na+-glucose). Antiporter terminology is unverified in this section.

Example 'pancreas secretes insulin' via exocytosis is not in reference; reference lists 'neurotransmitters, hormones, mucus' generally (line 154). Insulin is a hormone so consistent, but specific pancreas/insulin example is not cited.
Kidney/nephron/aquaporin reabsorption example is not present in the reference M3 section. Reference mentions aquaporins as channels (line 136) but does not connect them to kidneys.
Neuron action-potential wave / axonal propagation detail is not in the reference, which only states the Na/K pump 'create[s] the electrochemical gradient used by nerve cells' (line 146).

summary: Core mechanism claims (Singer-Nicolson 1972, 3 Na+/2 K+/ATP, GLUT1, aquaporins, tonicity, endo/exocytosis types) all verified; hook stats (100 Hz cycling, 20% energy, millions per cell) and several downstream physiology examples (kidney, insulin, antiporters, Ca2+/Cl-) are unverified by the reference.
